articles.name: How do I set up my AI agent to use help center content?
articles.title: How do I set up my AI agent to use help center content?
articles.body:
Question

How do I use an AI agent to generate answers from my help center content? Are the recommended articles based on the organizations that the end users belong to? How can I make sure my articles will appear in AI? How can I create content for AI?

Answer

AI agents respect the end user's access permissions in the help center every time. The AI agent bases AI-created replies and article recommendations on the user segment of the viewer.

Step 1: Create and enable your help center and articles

Before you publish an AI agent, prepare your help center:

  1. Create your help center
  2. Create your categories
  3. Create your sections, within these categories
  4. Create articles, within each section
  5. Ensure your article content follows best practices
  6. Ensure the Visible to field of your article settings shows Everyone, if you want AI to be able to show that information to anyone
  7. Ensure your articles are published
  8. Customize the theme of your help center
  9. Activate your help center   

Step 2: Restrict access to articles for AI with user segments

User segments added to your article settings restrict AI agent access to content

To hide an article from AI agents

  1. Create user segments 
  2. In your article settings, apply that user segments under the Visible to field
  3. You can bulk update articles, too

If your user belongs to 1 user segment but you have multiple user segments

  • The user is in the Premium Customers user segment and is authenticated in messaging
  • The AI agent will surface only articles visible to Everyone and Premium Customers
  • AI responses won't include additional restricted articles, for example, Enterprise Features

If you use no user segments in your article settings

  • Non-authenticated and anonymous end users will be able to access the article from your AI agent
  • The AI agent will only surface visible to everyone, which means public articles without restrictions

The difference between signed-in, authenticated users compared to anonymous users in AI agents

  • For signed-in users, the AI uses their specifically assigned user segments in article settings to show content
  • For anonymous users that are not signed in or authenticated in messaging, the AI assumes no user segment and returns only publicly available help center articles

Step 3: Set up AI agents

To get started with AI agents

  1. Set up messaging, where your AI bot will live
  2. Use Getting started with AI agents. As of May 2026, Zendesk offers a single, unified AI agent experience. Advanced agentic capabilities are now unlocked across all Zendesk Suite and Support plans: 
  3. Use the guided, self-service setup flow to configure your AI agent for simpler use cases across email and messaging
  4. Create a use case or instruction that directs the AI agent to use help center content when it answers customer questions
  5. If needed, configure advanced capabilities, such as agentic logic, multi-step procedures, and external API integrations for more complex tasks
  6. Publish the AI agent
Note for existing customers: If you still use AI agents - Essential or legacy functionality that includes bot builder, Zendesk will stop technical development for these tools by August 31, 2026. End-of-life and full service shut-off will occur on December 10, 2026. Complete your migration to the new experience by August 31 to avoid disruptions.

Understand AI agent terminology

What is the difference between Instructions and Use cases?
  • Instructions are general company policies that apply to everything
  • Use cases are department-specific procedures that apply only to specific situations
Instructions refer to
  • System-level prompts that define the AI agent's overall behavior
  • Examples include "Be friendly and professional", "Offer to escalate to a human agent every time the customer is frustrated", and "Use simple language, and avoid jargon"
  • Sets the overall tone and personality
  • Applies to all conversations
Use cases refer to
  • Specific scenarios that the AI agent handles
  • Examples include Password reset requests, billing questions, and product feature explanations
  • Each use case can have unique logic to address issues
  • The AI routes to the appropriate use case based on specific questions from customers
Deflection
  • The AI agent fully resolves a customer question without human agent involvement
  • Successful deflection means a ticket is prevented
  • Deflection rate is the metric for the percentage of conversations that the AI resolves
Escalation
  • The AI agent transfers the conversation to a human agent
  • This happens when the AI can't answer, the customer explicitly requests a human, or the system detects a complex issue
  • Escalation rate is the metric for the percentage of transferred conversations
Handoff
  • The exact moment a conversation transfers from the AI to a human agent
  • This includes full conversation history so the human agent has immediate context
  • A customer request, an AI decision, or a session timeout can trigger this action
Knowledge base
  • This is the collection of your help center articles
  • The AI agent searches this knowledge base to find and construct answers
  • A more complete knowledge base results in better AI performance

Step 4: Test user segment behavior

To verify the AI agent respects user segments:

  1. Create a test user account assigned to a specific user segment
  2. Sign in as that test user
  3. Ask the AI agent a question about restricted content
  4. Verify the following if AI suggests articles the user can see. The AI shouldn't suggest articles the user can't see

  5. A common issue occurs when the AI agent suggests a restricted article to the wrong user. This occurs because the article's user segment restriction isn't configured correctly. To fix this issue, review the Visible to settings for articles in Knowledge

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    How do I set up my AI agent or chatbot to use help center content or knowledge base help articles? How do I use an AI agent or answer bot to generate answers from my help center content? How can I make sure my articles will appear in AI? How can I create content for AI?

    To configure your AI agent or chatbot to use help articles, follow these steps:

    1. Create your help center or knowledge base.
    2. Create your categories.
    3. Create your sections within these categories.
    4. Create articles within each section.
    5. Ensure your article content follows best practices.
    6. Ensure the Visible to field of your article settings shows Everyone if you want AI to show that information to any visitor or customer.
    7. Ensure your articles are published.
    8. Customize the theme of your help center.
    9. Activate your help center.
    10. Use the guided, self-service setup flow to configure your AI agent across email and messaging.
    11. Create a use case or instruction that directs the AI agent to use help center content when it answers customer questions.
    12. Configure advanced capabilities like agentic logic, multi-step procedures, and external API integrations if needed.
    13. Publish the AI agent.

     

    Are the recommended articles based on the organizations or companies that the end users belong to? Does the AI agent use customer accounts to recommend help articles?

    No. The AI agent or chatbot bases AI-created replies and article recommendations on the user segment of the viewer. AI agents respect the end user's access permissions in the help center or knowledge base every time.

    How do I restrict access to articles for AI with user segments? How do I hide an article from AI agents or chatbots?

    To hide an article from AI agents, restrict access using these steps:

    1. Create user segments.
    2. Apply that user segment under the Visible to field in your article settings.
    You can also bulk update articles to apply these restrictions.

     

    What is the difference between signed-in authenticated users compared to anonymous users or visitors in AI agents? How does the chatbot handle permissions for logged-in clients versus anonymous visitors?

    For signed-in users, the AI uses their specifically assigned user segments in article settings to show content. For example, if an authenticated messaging user belongs to the Premium Customers user segment, the AI agent surfaces only articles visible to Everyone and Premium Customers. For anonymous users or visitors who are not signed in or authenticated in messaging, the AI assumes no user segment and returns only publicly available help center articles.

    What plans support advanced agentic capabilities? Is the unified AI agent experience available on my suite version or pricing tier?

    Zendesk offers a single, unified AI agent experience. Advanced agentic capabilities are unlocked across all Zendesk Suite and Support plans or ticketing systems.

    What is the difference between Instructions and Use cases? What are instructions and use cases for chatbots?

    Instructions are general company policies that define the AI agent's overall behavior, tone, and personality across all conversations. Use cases are department-specific procedures that apply to specific scenarios, such as password reset requests, billing questions, or product feature explanations. The AI routes to the appropriate use case based on the customer's specific question, and each use case can have unique logic.

    How do I test user segment behavior for my AI agent? How do I verify the chatbot respects permissions and access levels?

    To verify the AI agent or answer bot respects user segments, follow these steps:

    1. Create a test user account or customer account assigned to a specific user segment.
    2. Sign in as that test user.
    3. Ask the AI agent a question about restricted content.
    4. Verify if the AI suggests articles the user can see. The AI must not suggest articles the user cannot see.

     

    Why does the AI agent or chatbot suggest a restricted article to the wrong user or customer? How do I fix the chatbot showing restricted help articles to the wrong audience?

    This issue occurs because the article's user segment restriction is incorrect. To fix this issue, review the Visible to settings for articles in Knowledge.

    When is the end-of-life and full service shut-off for AI agents - Essential or legacy functionality? When will Zendesk stop technical development for the legacy bot builder?

    Zendesk stops technical development for AI agents - Essential and legacy functionality including bot builder by August 31, 2026. The official end-of-life and full service shut-off occurs on December 10, 2026. Complete your migration to the new experience by August 31, 2026 to avoid disruptions.

articles.name: Zendesk QA error: Something went wrong (500)
articles.title: Zendesk QA error: Something went wrong (500)
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When I edit or save a review, I get an error: Something went wrong (500) or Invalid assignment review request: invalid conversation for assignment.

Resolution steps

To resolve these error messages:

  1. Update your browser and clear your cache a cookies, or try a different browser to see if the issue persists.
  2. If possible, submit a review for a different conversation to see if the error is specific to one review.
  3. Follow the steps in: Using Zendesk QA as a reviewer.
  4. If you change a QA category after you submit the review, you can't edit that review. In that case, delete the review and recreate it.
  5. If this message appears: Max message size limit exceeded, divide the information into two sections.

  6. If the issue persists, click: Copy helpful information. I Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why do I get a "Something went wrong (500)" error when I edit or save a review? Why do I see an "Invalid assignment review request: invalid conversation for assignment" message when I edit or save a review? How do I fix Zendesk QA assignment review errors?

    To resolve these error messages, update your browser and clear your cache and cookies, or try a different browser to see if the issue persists. If possible, submit a review for a different conversation to check if the error is specific to one review. You can also follow the steps in: Using Zendesk QA as a reviewer. If the issue persists, click Copy helpful information and contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

    Can I edit a QA category after I submit a review? Can you change a QA category after a review is submitted?

    No. If you change a QA category after you submit the review, you cannot edit that review. In that case, delete the review and recreate it.

    What should I do if a "Max message size limit exceeded" error message appears in Zendesk QA? How do I fix the maximum message size limit error?

    If this message appears: "Max message size limit exceeded", divide the information into two sections.

articles.name: I can't access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension
articles.title: I can't access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When I try to access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension, I see a blank page.

Resolution steps

You may have used a previous login method tied to a former Klaus account to sign in to the Zendesk QA Chrome extension. Now, the extension may not load, show a blank page, or crash your browser.

To authenticate the extension again, follow these steps:

  • Clear your browser’s cached data and cookies for All time
  • Restart the browser, then load the Zendesk QA extension again
  • Select Continue with Zendesk
  • Enter your Zendesk subdomain, then click Sign in

For more information about this extension, see this article: Setting up and using the Zendesk QA Chrome browser extension.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I see a blank page in the Zendesk QA Chrome extension? Why does the Zendesk QA Chrome extension show a blank page? Why does the Zendesk QA Chrome extension crash my browser? How do I fix the Zendesk QA Chrome extension blank page?

You see a blank page or face browser crashes when you use a previous login method tied to a former Klaus account to sign in to the Zendesk QA Chrome extension. To authenticate the extension again, clear your browser's cached data and cookies for All time. Restart the browser, then load the Zendesk QA extension again. Select Continue with Zendesk, enter your Zendesk subdomain or brand, then click Sign in.

articles.name: Why do my agent statuses differ between the assignee ticket field and Analytics?
articles.title: Why do my agent statuses differ between the assignee ticket field and Analytics?
articles.body:
Question

I noticed that some agents do not appear as Online on the live dashboards in Analytics (formerly known as Explore). However, these agents show a green dot next to their username in the Assignee ticket field of tickets. Why does this happen?

Answer

Agents will not appear Online when the agent status is Transfers only.

  • The live dashboards in Analytics do not show your team members as Online, but the Assignee ticket field shows a green dot for both Online and Transfers only.

articles.name: Chat server error: Try to refresh chat now
articles.title: Chat server error: Try to refresh chat now
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

I received the Chat server error:Try to refresh chat now. If that doesn't work, ask your Admin to contact Zendesk with this error code, with a string of random letters and numbers. Why is this issue happening?

Resolution steps

The Chat server error indicates a recent subdomain change on the account. Live chat experiences up to two hours of downtime after a subdomain change.

  • The error should resolve itself within the two-hour window, see the article: Renaming your subdomain.
  • However, if you are still receiving this error and the subdomain hasn't been updated recently, troubleshoot your Chat connection.

articles.name: Can I create content only accessible to my AI agents?
articles.title: Can I create content only accessible to my AI agents?
articles.body:
Question

Can I create content only accessible to my AI agents?

Answer

No, an AI agent will only reference content that the end user has permission to view based on their user segment.

For more information, see Using restricted help center content in AI agent responses and Connecting knowledge sources to power generative replies in AI agents.

articles.name: I can't access the page for custom queues for omnichannel routing
articles.title: I can't access the page for custom queues for omnichannel routing
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

I can't access the page for custom queues for omnichannel routing. I used to see the custom queues option, but I cannot access it anymore.

Resolution steps

  1. Ensure that you are still an admin on the account. If you aren't, contact an admin and request them to provide you with admin permissions
  2. Ensure you use the Zendesk Suite Professional plan or above. If you trialed any of these plans or you downgraded, you can no longer access this panel. Custom queues are only available for Suite Professional and higher plans

articles.name: Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?
articles.title: Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?
articles.body:
Question

Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?

Answer

A 5-second delay is expected if one of the following functions is enabled in voice settings:

  • IVR
  • Callback
  • Call recording consent
  • Voicemail

If you'd like to avoid a 5-second wait time after the Voicemail and Callback greetings, you can contact Zendesk Customer Support and ask to disable the delay for your account.
