articles.name: Workflow: How can I use OAuth to authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions?
articles.title: Workflow: How can I use OAuth to authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions?
articles.body:
To authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions you must create a connection, such as what is described in Creating connections to integrate with external services. 

This workflow includes the steps to achieve this below: 

  • Step 1: Create the Zendesk OAuth client
  • Step 2: Add Zendesk client as a Connections client
  • Step 3: Create the Connection
  • Step 4: Use the Connection in a Custom Action

Step 1: Create the Zendesk OAuth client

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select APIs > OAuth clients 
  • Click Add OAuth client on the right side of the OAuth client list
  • Set the Client kind to Confidential
  • Set the redirect URL to https://zis.zendesk.com/api/services/zis/connections/oauth/callback (note that the subdomain is always zis, not your account subdomain)
  • Click Save. After the page refreshes, a new pre-populated Secret field appears on the lower side. This is the client_secret value specified in the OAuth 2.0 spec.
  • Copy the Secret value to your clipboard and save it somewhere safe. Note that the characters may extend past the width of the text box, so ensure to select everything before copying. For security reasons, your secret displays fully only once. After clicking Save, you only have access to the first nine characters.
  • For a full description of the fields mentioned in this step, see Using OAuth authentication with your application

Step 2: Add Zendesk client as a Connections client

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select Connections > OAuth Clients
  • Click Add client on the right side of the Connections OAuth client list
  • Set the OAuth grant type to Authorization code
  • Add the client ID and secret from your Zendesk client created in Step 1
  • Set the Authorize URL to: https://{your_subdomain}.zendesk.com/oauth/authorizations/new
  • Set the Token URL to: https://{your_subdomain}.zendesk.com/oauth/tokens
  • Enter a space-separate list of default scopes. Scopes are permissions that represent what a client application can access on behalf of the user. Note that it is recommended to only set the minimum necessary scopes for your end goal (avoid using the blanket read/write unless necessary). The available scopes are documented here.
  • Click Save to create the client

Step 3: Create the Connection

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select Connections > OAuth Clients
  • Click Create connection
  • Set the Authentication type to OAuth2.0
  • Set the scopes you want for this specific connection, or leave blank to inherit the default scopes from the client
  • Set the Allowed Domain to your Zendesk account URL {your_subdomain}.zendesk.com

  • After you click save, you go through the popup OAuth authorization screen. Click Allow.

Step 4: Use the Connection in a Custom Action

  • At this point, the connection you created in step 3 is available in the Authentication dropdown when you create a custom action
  • Tip: Keep in mind that if you edit an existing custom action to include this authorization, you must re-add it to any action flows where it is used. Changes to custom actions do not automatically reflect in any action flows that include it.

articles.name: Service Incident - July 1, 2026 - Sunshine Conversations | Multiple Pods - Malformed AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels
articles.title: Service Incident - July 1, 2026 - Sunshine Conversations | Multiple Pods - Malformed AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels
articles.body:
Summary

On June 25, 2026 from 14:59 UTC to July 1, 2026 13:46 UTC, customers using social messaging channels (such as WhatsApp) experienced an issue where the “ask for details” form displayed broken or missing labels, which made it difficult (and in some cases impossible) for end-users to complete and submit support requests.

Timeline

July 01, 2026 09:17 UTC | July 01, 2026 2:17 AM PT 

We’re actively investigating an issue impacting customer's end-users using the AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels app such as WhatsApp. We’re working on a fix and will share another update as soon as we have more information.

July 01, 2026 12:29 UTC | July 01, 2026 5:29 AM PT 

Our Engineering team is currently deploying a fix for this issue. We’ll update you once the deployment is complete. Thank you for your patience as we work to resolve it.

July 01, 2026 13:51 UTC | July 01, 2026 6:51 AM PT 

Our engineers have released and tested a fix for the issue impacting AI Agent "ask for details" forms. The issue is now resolved and all forms should behave as expected. Thank you for your patience while we worked to resolve this incident.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a gap in our release process that allowed an update to be published without all of the required language resources for the “ask for details” form. Because those label files were missing, the form could not display its text correctly and appeared broken for customers using social messaging channels.

Resolution

To fix this issue, we built and deployed an updated version of the affected component that correctly included the missing language/label resources. Once the updated version finished deploying across our environments, the form labels returned to normal and customers confirmed the experience was working again.

Remediation Items

  1. Add monitoring and alerts for missing language files so issues like broken or missing labels are detected quickly.
  2. Create a documented recovery process for restoring missing language files so we can fix this kind of problem faster and more consistently.
  3. Add automated checks to detect out-of-date translations to reduce the chance of incomplete or stale text appearing in customer-facing forms.
  4. Improve form layout resilience when text is wrong or unusually long so buttons and key actions stay visible and usable even if labels render incorrectly.
  5. Update the release pipeline to fail the build when required language files are missing so incomplete releases can’t be published

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-07-03
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-07-03
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Voice
  • Knowledge
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • App Marketplace
  • Products with no updates this week

Voice

New:

  • Admins can now restrict outbound calling to specific groups of agents. See Managing phone line settings.

Fixed:

  • Fixed a bug in which calls were reported as assigned to the default group even when the line was external. In fact, external numbers cannot receive calls, so the group should be "NULL". This affected only omnichannel contact center customers.

Knowledge

New:

  • The Zendesk connector can now ingest articles from private help centers
  • The Zendesk connector supports PDFs as article attachments. To get a feature, enroll in the EAP.
  • Box and S3 support PDFs as files. To get a feature, enroll in the EAP.
  • The Knowledge copilot recommendations section now includes a "View all recommendations" link that takes you straight to your full list of article and procedure recommendations in Admin Center.

AI agents - Advanced

New:

  • Zendesk schedules are now supported in AI agents, allowing the exclusion of holidays from regular working hours when checking agent availability. See Checking business hours and agent availability during AI agent conversations and Configuring escalation strategies and flows for AI agents.

Analytics

New:

  • Customer satisfaction (CSAT) ratings are now available in the real-time monitoring ticket progress dashboard. This enhancement allows supervisors and team leads to monitor customer satisfaction performance alongside ticket workflows, enabling faster responses to customer sentiment and more informed team management decisions.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Clonepartner (Support)
    • Clonepartner delivers a fully customizable migration tailored to your exact data structures. Migrate your tickets, contacts, and custom fields from any help desk with zero downtime. Run precise point-in-time migrations for the delta to ensure a seamless transition. Connect your account securely via OAuth and Clonepartner will handle the heavy lifting.
  • PII Shield (Support) (paid)
    • PII Shield scans Zendesk tickets for sensitive personal information and gives agents the tools to permanently remove it — before it's shared, stored, or audited. All detection runs in the agent's browser. Ticket content is never sent to external servers, never processed by third-party AI, and never stored outside Zendesk. This is a genuine compliance advantage for regulated industries. After every redaction, PII Shield posts a structured internal note to the ticket recording what was removed, which agent removed it, and the exact timestamp. This note is your durable audit trail.
  • Netgsm SMS (Support)
    • Netgsm SMS allows your support agents to send SMS messages directly from Zendesk tickets and user profiles without leaving the support interface. Send SMS to customers directly from any ticket or user sidebar. Select from your registered sender names and view SMS delivery status in real time.
  • WitWave by Witbor (Support)
    • WitWave by Witbor closes the gap in messaging conversations: your agents can listen, visualize, and send WhatsApp voice notes directly from the ticket interface, without opening external apps and without losing context. Play back previous voice notes of the conversation with an interactive waveform, directly from the ticket. Record new voice notes from the ticket panel and send them to the customer in a single click. WitWave detects whether the ticket is on the WhatsApp channel and alerts the agent before recording, preventing send errors.
  • KnowledgeBridge (Support)
    • KnowledgeBridge automatically generates llms.txt and llms-full.txt files from your Zendesk Help Center, making your knowledge base immediately accessible to AI systems, LLM-powered agents, and automated tooling. With one click, KnowledgeBridge fetches all published articles from your Help Center, converts them to clean markdown, and assembles two machine-readable output files, llms.txt and llms-full.txt

Products with no updates this week

  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • AI agents
  • Support
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Service Incident - June 26, 2026 - Sell | All Pods - Deals feature outage in Zendesk Sales and SQL errors due to unintentional dropped DB tables
articles.title: Service Incident - June 26, 2026 - Sell | All Pods - Deals feature outage in Zendesk Sales and SQL errors due to unintentional dropped DB tables
articles.body:
Summary

Between June 26, 2026, 17:37 UTC and June 28, 2026, 03:45 UTC, Zendesk Sales customers across multiple pods experienced an outage and degradation of the Deals feature. Issues included problems loading, saving, or creating Deals, blank pages, and missing data, occurring while database restoration was underway.

Timeline

June 26, 2026 19:03 UTC | June 26, 2026 12:03 PM PT

We are investigating an issue preventing some Zendesk Sales customers from accessing features within the Sales product, including Deals. We will provide an update as soon as new information is available.

June 26, 2026 19:53 UTC | June 26, 2026 12:53 PM PT

Our engineers are continuing to work towards restoring access to all Sales product features. We appreciate your patience and understanding. We will provide an update by the next hour or sooner if we have new information to share.

June 26, 2026 22:54 UTC | June 26, 2026 3:54 PM PT

Our engineers continue to work to restore the impacted Sales product features, with a focus on the most impactful.  We appreciate your patience while we work on this, we will provide an update within the next 3 hours or sooner if we have new information to share.

June 26, 2026 23:23 UTC | June 26, 2026 4:23 PM PT

The ability to save new deals has been restored and users should be able to operate as expected. Our engineers will continue to work towards restoring any lost data during the outage.  Please let us know ASAP if you are still seeing impact. Thank you for your patience as we resolve this issue.

June 27, 2026 3:50 UTC | June 26, 2026 8:50 PM PT

Our engineering team is actively working to restore all data impacted by the outage, including custom field data. We sincerely appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to fully stabilize the system. We will keep you informed of any new developments as they become available.

June 27, 2026 5:27 UTC | June 26, 2026 10:27 PM PT

Our Engineering team is actively working to restore the data affected, including custom field data. Please check your system and let us know if you continue to experience issues viewing the custom field data. We appreciate your patience as our team works diligently to fully resolve this matter.

June 28, 2026 5:05 UTC | June 27, 2026 10:05 PM PT

Thank you for your patience while we worked towards resolution. We have now completed the full remediation of the impacted data, including the custom field data. All functionality has been restored, we will continue to monitor through the weekend. Please let us know if you are still seeing any impact to your systems, data, or functionality, thank you.

July 1, 2026 5:56 UTC | June 30, 2026 10:56 PM PT

We’ve completed the full remediation of the impacted data, and functionality has been fully restored, including the ability to view and save new deals.

Root Cause Analysis

The incident was caused by an accidental command during maintenance that unintentionally deleted seven important tables in the Sales database, resulting in errors and an outage of the Deals feature until the data was restored from backup.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, the seven accidentally deleted Sales database tables were restored from a recent backup. Data was carefully reconciled to prevent conflicts, and missing information was replenished through a controlled recovery process. The team closely monitored system health and customer tickets until full access and functionality were confirmed.

Remediation Items

  1. Review and update user permissions, removing unnecessary "Drop" privileges.
  2. Increase team awareness by defining clear environment-specific access controls.
  3. Review and regulate activities scheduled on Fridays to reduce risk.
  4. Implement safeguards to prevent harmful database commands from being executed in the production environment.
  5. Investigate and resolve the intermittent issue affecting the 'design1st' customer’s deal field updates.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Announcing the Employee Service AI Agents (EAP)
articles.title: Announcing the Employee Service AI Agents (EAP)
articles.body:
Zendesk is excited to introduce a new AI agent experience designed for employee service. This update brings AI-native support directly to your employees, helping resolve internal requests faster and more securely within their existing workflows.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

The Employee Service AI Agents EAP delivers conversational AI-based support directly to your employees, helping resolve internal requests faster and more securely within their existing workflows.

When the employee service (ES) AI agent is enabled on an account, when employees visit your company help center, the traditional search bar experience shifts to an interactive conversation with the AI agent. This AI agent automatically connects to your company's knowledge to deliver instant answers to your employees.

Key features of the ES AI Agents EAP include:

  • Automatic activation: The ES AI agent experience turns on automatically for Employee Service Suite accounts with the following exceptions: HIPAA or FedRAMP certified accounts, accounts opted out of generative AI, and accounts that already have an active AI agent solution.
  • Enterprise search: You can connect your internal knowledge from key systems with pre-built connectors to common sources: Google Drive, SharePoint, Confluence, Notion, Slack, Teams, Box, Seismic, and a Web Crawler. We will introduce additional connectors later this year.
  • Secure knowledge: AI agents respect existing content permissions at the source, ensuring employees only see what they’re allowed to see, no matter which system the information comes from.
  • Employee channels: The AI agent can be integrated with Slack and Teams, so your employees can reach out for and receive support where they’re already working.
  • AI agent actions: Soon after the EAP begins, an update will allow you to connect the employee service AI agent to action flows to seamlessly handle tasks across Zendesk and third-party services, resolving requests end-to-end.

This EAP will be available on the Employee Service Suite only, but will expand to include eligible customers using our Customer Service Suite for internal service when the product becomes generally available.

A note on pricing and credit structures: During the EAP, the ES AI agents are free to use. When the feature becomes generally available, resolutions using connected external sources might count toward your automated resolution credits and totals.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Zendesk has heard your feedback that traditional customer-facing AI tools often fall short for internal teams such as IT, HR, Finance, and Operations. Internal employee support requires managing fragmented information across various enterprise systems while strictly maintaining security and role-based data permissions. 

The Employee Service AI agent experience addresses those barriers directly, allowing you to deliver fast, secure, and automated internal support right where your employees already work.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. This EAP is being turned on automatically for all eligible Employee Service Suite accounts. Exceptions include HIPAA or FedRAMP certified accounts, accounts opted out of generative AI, and accounts that already have an active AI agent solution.

To customize your employee service AI agent experience, you can:

  • Connect external enterprise knowledge sources: Box, Confluence, Google Drive, Notion, Seismic, SharePoint, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Web crawler.
  • Extend support to collaborative channels that your employees use daily, such as Slack or Microsoft Teams.

Prior to the launch of the EAP, you can opt out of this automatic activation. To do so, contact Zendesk Customer Support and include "Opt out of Employee Service AI Agent EAP" in your message. After the launch of the EAP, you can disconnect the Employee Service AI agent from your help center at any time.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - Social Messaging Channels | Multiple Pods - Issue with Social Messaging Channels
articles.title: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - Social Messaging Channels | Multiple Pods - Issue with Social Messaging Channels
articles.body:
Summary

On June 12, 2026, between 13:33 UTC and 16:30 UTC, Social Messaging customers across multiple pods experiencing an outage with Meta channels including WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Instagram Messenger. During the outage, users were unable to send or receive messages on these channels.

Timeline

June 12, 2026 14:24 UTC | 7:24 AM PT
Our engineers are investigating the issue impacting social messaging channels. At the moment this appears to be a widespread Meta outage, and we will provide further updates when we hear more information from Meta. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

June 12, 2026 14:56 UTC | 07:56 AM PT
We are still working with Meta on resolving the issue impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram messaging. We will provide an update as soon as we have new information to share.

June 12, 2026 16:27 UTC | 9:27 AM PT
We unfortunately do not have any substantive updates to share from Meta while we monitor the situation impacting Meta social messaging channels. We will be sure to share any news we can as soon as it is available.

June 12, 2026 17:36 UTC | 10:36 AM PT
We are pleased to hear that Meta has resolved the issue that was impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram social messaging channels. Our own monitoring shows full recovery. Thank you.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an outage with a Social Messaging Channels provider.

Resolution

The issue was fixed by our partner Social Messaging Channels provider.

 

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-06-26
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-06-26
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • Voice
  • Knowledge
  • App Marketplace
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Products with no updates this week

Admin Center

New

  • The content of the email that gets sent when accessing your account when your SSO provider is down has been updated to be simpler, clearer, and more closely aligned with our content standards. 

Copilot

New:

  • Zendesk is making AI more accessible by bringing the Copilot add-ons’ intelligent triage classification to Suite and Support Professional plans and above. This feature uses machine learning to automatically analyze and classify incoming tickets, helping you understand your top ticket drivers without manual effort.
  • Auto assist will no longer suggest solving a ticket unless it is asked to do so in a procedure.

Voice

Fixed:

  • Fixed an issue where click-to-call from the Web Widget did not connect when Help Center authentication was enabled.

Knowledge

New:

  • You can now connect Amazon S3 to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used. See Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Ticket Done (Support) (paid)
    • Ticket Done brings AI assistance directly into your Zendesk ticket sidebar so agents spend less time searching and more time solving. Ticket Done reads each incoming ticket and instantly surfaces AI-generated responses sourced from your actual Zendesk Help Center articles. No generic AI. No hallucinations. Every suggestion is grounded in your documentation. Generate a customer-ready reply in seconds. Ticket Done reads the ticket, searches your knowledge base using semantic AI, and drafts a response your agent can review and send. Need internal guidance before responding? Get Help surfaces the right policies, procedures, or escalation paths — visible only to the agent, never the customer.
  • Fusion (Theme)
    • Fusion is a premium Zendesk Guide theme that combines modern design, powerful functionality, and seamless navigation to help customers find answers faster. Designed for businesses of all sizes, Fusion transforms your help center into a professional support hub that enhances customer satisfaction and reduces support tickets. With its clean layouts, advanced search experience, and highly customizable design, Fusion delivers the perfect balance of aesthetics and usability. 

Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)

New:

  • The forecast vs actual page (EAP) now has a more granular CSV export.

  • New forecast variance metric that compares the volume forecasted with the actual volume received.

    Details on both can be found in Understanding and using the forecast vs actual page (EAP)

  • The default time off report has a new billable hours metric. It reflects the net number of paid hours lost as a result of the time-off request (Billable Hours = Total time-off duration – Duration of unpaid activities (within the same scheduled window)).  
  • Time off reasons can now be configured to be used by specific teams. More details in this article for Time off reasons: team visibility setting. 

Fixed:

  • Audit log events related to changes in performance boards and the extension tracking settings page will now appear in the WFM audit logs page.
  • The Neural Prophet forecasting algorithm is no longer available. Scenarios that were using it will have it changed to the Prophet algorithm on the next forecast recalculation.

Products with no updates this week

  • AI agents
  • Support
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center
articles.title: Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center
articles.body:
Admins can access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console from Contact Center to manage telephony and infrastructure settings. You can also review your Amazon Connect instance details in the Contact Center admin interface.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Admins can access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console from Contact Center to manage telephony and infrastructure settings. You can also review your Amazon Connect instance details in the Contact Center admin interface.

Related article:

  • About AWS services in Contact Center

To access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console

  1. Click the Zendesk product menu in the top bar, then select Contact center.
  2. Under Overview, review your Amazon Connect instance details.
  3. Click Amazon Connect Dashboard or AWS Console to open the related AWS service:
    • Amazon Connect Dashboard: View real-time metrics, manage contact center operations, configure routing profiles, and build contact flows.
    • AWS Console: Manage cloud infrastructure, user permissions, and other technical configuration settings.
Note: If you can't access Amazon Connect or the AWS Console, contact your administrator.

articles.name: Configuring call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.title: Configuring call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.body:
Summary: ◀▼

You can set up call monitoring so selected agents can listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and use the right workflow settings when joining. This requires adding the right real-time monitoring permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring notes and resolution codes in the Contact Center app, which helps you control call barge-in and monitor calls more effectively.

Admins can configure call monitoring for Contact Center by setting permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring workflows in the Contact Center app. Use this article if you want selected users to listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and configure workflows when joining calls.

Admins can configure call monitoring for Contact Center by setting permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring workflows in the Contact Center app. Use this article if you want selected users to listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and configure workflows when joining calls.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Configuring access permissions for call monitoring
  • Configuring workflows when joining calls

Related article:

  • Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center

Configuring access permissions for call monitoring

Before users can monitor and join active calls, you must assign them an Amazon Connect security profile that includes the required real-time monitoring permissions.

To give agents access to call monitoring
  1. In Amazon Connect, click Users > Security profiles.
  2. Click Add new security profile.
  3. Enter a name for the profile.
  4. Under Analytics and Optimization, select Real-time metrics and Real-time contact monitoring.
  5. Click Save.
  6. Click Users, then add the security profile to the agents who will monitor or join calls.

Configuring workflows when joining calls

The following options apply only to the Zendesk Contact Center app, not the native Zendesk voice experience.

To configure workflows when joining calls
  1. In Contact Center, click the profile button (), then click Admin settings.
  2. Click the Settings icon (), then click the Workflows tab.
  3. Find the workflow, then click Edit workflow.
  4. Configure the Notes and Resolution Codes, as shown.

articles.name: Managing calls in Contact Center
articles.title: Managing calls in Contact Center
articles.body:
Agents can manage calls using the call console and the call controls in the ticket. The call console is particularly useful when you navigate away from your ticket in the Agent Workspace as it has a link to the ticket as well as the call controls. You can place the call on hold, mute your microphone, transfer the call, make a conference call, and end the call.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

You can manage live calls in the call console or ticket controls by putting customers on hold, muting yourself, transferring calls, adding participants, or ending calls. Use hold to let customers wait with configured audio or updates, mute to block background noise, and transfer or conference calls when you need help from other agents, queues, or external contacts. Wrap-up and ticket notes finish the call.

Agents can manage calls using the call console and the call controls in the ticket. The call console is particularly useful when you navigate away from your ticket in the Agent Workspace as it has a link to the ticket as well as the call controls. You can place the call on hold, mute your microphone, transfer the call, make a conference call, and end the call.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Putting calls on hold or muting yourself
  • Transferring calls
  • Making a conference call
  • Ending a call

Related articles:

  • Accepting and declining calls
  • Making outbound calls
  • Monitoring and joining calls

Putting calls on hold or muting yourself

During a call, you might want to put the customer on hold or mute your microphone so you can consult with colleagues or search for information.

To put a call on hold

  • In the call console, click the Hold icon ();

  • Or, in the ticket call controls, click the place on hold icon ().

    When you put a customer on hold, they are moved into a system-controlled flow where they cannot hear you and you cannot hear the customer. Depending on your configuration, the customer will hear:

    • Custom Audio: Music or helpful tips relevant to their specific issue.

    • Information Loops: Periodic updates or reminders about required documents while they wait.

To mute yourself

  • In the call console, click the Mute icon ();

  • Or, in the ticket call controls click the mute microphone icon ().

    The customer hears nothing from your end, but you can still hear them.

    Use this for short pauses where you need to listen for the customer to finish a task or to prevent them from hearing background noise while you look up info.

Transferring calls

When you need to transfer a call, this will place the call on hold while the transfer occurs.

To transfer a call

  1. In the call console, click the Transfer icon () or, in the ticket call controls, click the transfer call icon ().

    The customer can still hear you.

  2. Select the agent, queue, external contact or dial the contact using the number pad.

    The options for transferring your call are as follows:

    • All: A list combining all Agents, Queues, and External numbers into one list.

    • Agents: A list of agents regardless of availability.

    • Queues: A list of the call queues available for receiving the transfer.

    • External: A list of pre-configured external telephone numbers.

    If you decide not to proceed with the transfer, click the close icon (X).

  3. Click Transfer.

    This will automatically put the customer on hold. For more information, see Putting calls on hold or muting yourself.

  4. (Optional) Do a warm transfer.

    While the customer is on hold, you can speak with the new participant. If needed, you can also add other participants before you leave the call, see Making a conference call.

  5. (Optional) Click the hold icon () next to the customer participant.

    Before you leave the call you can also speak with the customer.

  6. Click Leave or the leave icon () next to your name to leave the call.

    This will automatically put you in wrap-up mode and the customer will remain on the call with the other participants.

Making a conference call

You can add participants to an ongoing call.

To add participants to a call

  1. In the call console, click Add or in the ticket controls click the add icon ().

  2. Select the participant you want to add to the call or enter their number using the dial pad.

  3. Click Add participant.

    After you add the first participant, the customer is automatically placed on hold.

  4. (Optional) Repeat steps 2 and 3, as necessary.

  5. (Optional) Speak with the new participants before resuming the call with the customer.

  6. Click the hold icon () to take the customer off hold and resume the call.

Ending a call

You can end a call manually by hanging up. If the customer you're talking to hangs up first, the call ends automatically.

To end a call

  1. In the call console, click End or, in the ticket call controls, click the end icon ().

  1. Enter data on all required ticket fields and add notes to the ticket associated with the call.

  2. Click Finish wrap-up to conclude the work.

    If your agent settings have an after call work timeout, you can let that time run out to conclude the work.

    If configured, copilot will run and add data such as AI summarization, intent and sentiment to the ticket.

Note: Your status doesn’t change during the call but Zendesk will only offer you calls again after wrap-up. Depending on your focus mode setting, you might receive other non-call contacts. For more information, see Understanding focus mode.

articles.name: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.title: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.body:
Use this article if your AI agent doesn't respond as expected, escalates too early, or behaves differently across channels or instances. It covers the most common configuration and setup issues, along with the first checks to make before you contact Zendesk Customer Support.

Preliminary AI agent troubleshooting questions

  1. Is the AI agent enabled and assigned to the right channel? see Creating an AI agent to automatically resolve customer issues.
  2. Are relevant articles, knowledge sources, or integrations published and available to your customers? see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Optimizing your knowledge content for generative AI.
  3. Is the correct language activated and supported?
  4. Have you reviewed the conversation logs for errors, escalation, or no response?
    5. Note: When you answer those questions, you know whether you face a configuration issue, a content issue, a channel, or a language issue, or something that needs Zendesk Customer Support.

When you follow the advice above, review common issues below:

The AI agent doesn’t respond to questions

Common causes to a lack of replies from your AI agent

  • The agent isn’t connected to the channel
  • Articles or sources are unpublished or inaccessible
  • The language isn't active
  • A setup or integration step is incomplete

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm the agent is enabled, assigned to the correct channel, and connected to the right knowledge source. For more information, see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents.
  2. Check whether the language is active and supported

AI agent replies when it should hand off

Common causes

  • The fallback logic is too permissive
  • The procedure routes all ambiguous cases through the AI response step
  • Knowledge content is too broad to trigger a clean handoff

To resolve this issue:

  1. Review your escalation strategies and flows
  2. Verify your fallback behavior, and confidence or routing logic
  3. Test with weak-match examples
AI agent escalates too early or shows a technical error

Common causes

  • Inputs are missing or malformed
  • Flow logic doesn’t handle edge cases well
  • Integration errors or parameter mismatches
  • An unhandled condition in the procedure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check your required parameters and fallback handling
  2. Test the integration in the integration builder and check that the request parameters match
  3. Check the HTTP status code
  4. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  5. Verify the conversation logs for differences between successful and failed cases

For more information, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Knowledge sources are missing, incomplete, or inconsistent

Common causes

  • The help center is not active and published
  • Articles are unpublished or their Visible to field does not allow customers and the AI agent to access the content
  • The source was not imported correctly
  • Different instances are on different rollout states. The feature is gated or not yet available everywhere. Search rules are not logical, labels are missing, or incorrect operators are used.

To resolve this issue:

  1. If you use articles from the Zendesk help center, ensure that the help center is active and published
  2. Ensure that article settings are published and the Placement > Visible to field is set to Everyone
  3. Confirm the knowledge sources were added in the right place, imported successfully, and is accessible to the AI agent. Verify imported knowledge sources for AI agents.
  4. Verify if the crawl failed because it timed out, is missing pages, or the crawl includes the right pages but the wrong content. see Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents and Using a web crawler to index external content.
  5. Compare configuration search rules between instances. for AI agents Advanced only
  6. Check for restricted articles and where the end user does not have access
AI agent behaves differently by language

Common causes to multiple responses for each language

  • The language hasn’t been activated
  • The agent is falling back because it can’t detect or serve the target language
  • The content exists, but not in the expected language

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm that the target language is activated
  2. Language detection is configured
  3. Content exists in the correct language
AI agent integration is failing

Common causes to a failed app integration with AI agents

  • Invalid credentials
  • Unsupported payload format
  • Endpoint or mapping errors
  • External system timeout or failure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check the connection
  2. Verify the authentication and conversation logs
  3. Test the integration in the integration builder
  4. Check that the request parameters match and the HTTP status code
  5. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  6. Test the integration in the builder if available

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Common causes to a failed CRM connectivity

  • The bot doesn’t join conversations
  • Escalations to agents fail
  • Tagging errors occur during CRM-based flow handling

To resolve this issue:

  1. Verify whether the bot joins conversations
  2. Ensure that agent escalations and human hand offs succeed
  3. Verify if tags are applied correctly

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents.

When to contact Zendesk Customer Support about AI agents

Contact Zendesk Customer Support if:

  • The issue persists after you carefully verify your configuration against this article
  • The behavior is inconsistent across instances
  • You can replicate the issue
  • Logs show an error you can't resolve
  • The issue appears to be a product bug or backend provisioning problem

If you need help to submit a request, see How to contact Customer Support.

Before you contact Zendesk Customer Support

Gather the following details to assist with our investigation:

  • Your Zendesk subdomain or URL
  • The AI agent name
  • Channel
  • Conversation or ticket ID
  • Time of the issue
  • Steps to reproduce
  • Screenshots or screen recording
  • Relevant conversation logs
  • Any related integration details

Additional articles

  • Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents
  • Configuring escalation strategies and flows for AI agents
  • Configuring language detection for AI agents
  • Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents
  • Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents (AI agents - Advanced only)

articles.name: What's new in Zendesk: July 2026
articles.title: What's new in Zendesk: July 2026
articles.body:
Click Follow in the What's New section to be notified each month when the What's New is published.

Check out what's new in the last month:

  • Support
  • Accounts and billing
  • AI agents
  • Copilot
  • Security
  • Knowledge
  • Messaging
  • Voice
  • Contact Center
  • Workforce management
  • Quality assurance
  • Apps and integrations
  • Developers

Also don't miss:

  • Zendesk early access programs
  • What's new videos on YouTube

Support

  • Select multiple custom roles and view their permissions side-by-side in a comparison matrix, making it easier to review role differences and update role settings without opening each role individually. See Comparing custom roles.
  • Filter tickets by the requester's email using "Is" and "Is not" operators, with email format validation to prevent errors. This enhances ticket organization and routing. See Creating views to build customized lists of tickets.
  • AI-based predictive routing in omnichannel routing uses AI to assign messaging tickets to the agents predicted to resolve them fastest, improving resolution times and agent efficiency. To help measure the success of predictive routing, a new metric called Agent Engagement Time tracks agent activity. See Using predictive routing in queues.
  • The new search side panel centralizes search tabs and saved searches from the top toolbar to a left-side panel, unifying the search experience and allowing for future expansion of search capabilities. See Searching Zendesk Support data.

  • Agents can listen to audio messages attached to email comments directly within Zendesk without downloading files. This enhances support efficiency and accessibility. See Playing back audio comments in email and messaging tickets.

Accounts and billing

  • Digital customers with limited ARR can temporarily pause their accounts. As an alternative to cancelling your account, Zendesk allows eligible customers to pause their account for up to three months. See Pausing accounts.

AI agents

  • Forethought AI agents by Zendesk are now available for purchase as an add-on, offering autonomous customer inquiry resolution, intent identification, task automation, agent assistance, insights, and quality review. See About Forethought AI agents by Zendesk.
  • You can now directly edit generative procedure maps. Every time you create or edit a generative procedure, an AI-generated procedure map is produced. If you need to make targeted updates to the procedure map, you can directly edit it instead of relying on the AI generation. See Editing the procedure map.
  • Use cases can now be restricted so they're used only inside of dialogues. These use cases are triggered only inside dialogue flows and cannot be triggered during general conversation, such as when an AI agent is providing AI-generated answers. This helps ensure that use cases trigger only when they make sense. See Restricting a use case to be used only inside of a dialogue.

Copilot

  • Intelligent triage terminology has been updated to rename the "intent" classification to "topic" across the Copilot add-on. This is a naming change only with no impact on workflows or functionality.
  • Intelligent triage now includes guided onboarding to help you get started and create workflows based on your top customer trends. See About Intelligent triage.

Security

  • Use AI-generated, easy-to-understand descriptions for individual access log events, helping less-technical users quickly interpret complex log data and guiding investigations. See Using the access log to monitor agent activity.

Knowledge

  • You can now connect Google Drive, Amazon S3, and Box as external content sources to make the content available for help center search, generative search, AI agents, and Agent Workspace. See Connecting Google Drive as an external knowledge source,  Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source, and  Connecting Box as an external knowledge source.
  • Connect a second Zendesk account to your primary account to access content from multiple Zendesk accounts in one place. This enhances content reuse across advanced AI agents, search, and generative search. See Connecting Zendesk as an external knowledge source.

Messaging

  • Turn on end-user presence detection for messaging to identify if end users are still on your website or app or have abandoned the conversation, improving customer experience and reducing agent handle time. It uses presence signals to mark sessions as inactive when users leave, impacting queue and capacity management, and letting accounts automatically close or follow up on abandoned conversations. See Turning on end-user presence detection for messaging.
  • The Generated by AI disclosure redesign for social and mobile messaging channels integrates the "Generated by AI" label into the bot's display name for a cleaner conversational experience. It also introduces per-channel control via API to disable the AI label if desired. The redesigned label is applied automatically to mobile channels, and social channels need to confirm the update in their settings. To turn off the label per-channel, see the Update Integration API.

Voice

  • Set the number of times the IVR greeting repeats from one to three before routing to the default option, improving caller routing flexibility. The IVR settings page has also been refreshed with a modern look. See Routing incoming calls with IVR.
  • Admins can now restrict outbound calling by agent group in Voice (Talk). This lets you control which agent groups can use specific phone lines for outbound calls, helping reduce misrouted or costly calls. See Managing phone line settings.

Contact Center

  • The native Zendesk Contact Center is now generally available, integrated directly into the Zendesk Agent Workspace for a unified experience with single status management, call monitoring, simpler authentication, native settings, and expanded language support. See About Zendesk Contact Center.

Workforce management

  • WFM combined workstreams have been added to the Schedule page staffing panels. Combined workstreams now appear in Day view staffing panels, showing scheduled staffing without forecasting. This update helps users efficiently schedule and view staffing for combined workstreams. See Accessing staffing panels, menus, and charts.

  • Team visibility settings for time off reasons allow managers and admins to control which teams can see and use specific time off reasons, tailoring leave options to team-specific locations, perks, and policies. This change improves granularity by restricting time off reasons visibility to assigned teams only. See Creating and managing time off reasons for agents.

  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) is now available for Suite Professional plans and above at no extra cost. It provides visibility into agent activity and daily average handle time (AHT) for 24 hours, helping managers understand workload trends and improve planning. Agents can also see how much time they spend handling customer interactions. See Trying WFM for free using workforce activity tracking.

  • The out of adherence automation allows admins to configure automated real-time notifications that alert managers and agents the moment an agent is working on a task that doesn't match their current schedule. This change gives supervisors and managers the visibility they need to surface schedule deviations the moment they happen, enabling immediate course-correction rather than after-the-fact reporting. See About workforce management automations.

Quality assurance

  • The Coaching dashboard in Zendesk Quality Assurance (QA) provides an overview of coaching sessions, feedback reviews, and coaching impact on Internal Quality Score (IQS). It allows filtering by date, coach, and coachee, tracking session status, and reviewing coaching trends. See Accessing and viewing the Coaching dashboard in Zendesk QA.

Apps and integrations

  • New action builder connectors introduce five new integrations—HubSpot, Chargebee, Stripe, Calendly, and GitHub—into Zendesk action flows, enabling automation of CRM, billing, scheduling, and engineering tasks directly within Zendesk. See Action builder and action flows for a full list of connectors.

Developers

  • Use the behavioral customization APIs for Web Widget to programmatically control conversation flow and message handling in the Messaging Web Widget. This includes creating and fetching conversations, sending messages, navigating conversations, and intercepting messages. See Behavioral customization APIs for more details.

articles.name: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.title: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.body:
This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

Learn how to accept or decline Contact Center calls, set the right agent status, and use call details before answering. If you can’t take a call, decline it to send it to the next available agent and reduce customer wait time. You also need a supported browser and notifications turned on to get incoming call alerts.

This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

You must use a supported browser and, if you want your operating system to notify you of incoming calls, configure your browser for notifications.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Setting your status for calls
  • Accepting calls
  • Automatically accepting calls
  • Declining calls

Related articles:

  • Understanding how Contact Center calls become tickets

Setting your status for calls

To receive calls, you must set your status to a Zendesk status that maps to Available in Amazon Connect. The standard Zendesk status Transfer only isn't supported for Contact Center calls. For information about the Zendesk statuses and how to set your status, see Setting your agent status.

Tip: If you're an agent and don't know whether a given status maps to Available in Connect, ask your admin.

Accepting calls

To accept a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details to view any associated information.

    The information comes from the contact flow and its contact attributes.
  2. Click Accept.

    When you accept the call, depending on your settings, Zendesk might begin recording the call and create a ticket. For details, see how calls become tickets. A counter shows the duration of the call.

    You can still show or hide the call details at this point.

  3. Click the close icon (X) to close the call notification and proceed with the call.

Automatically accepting calls

When admins configure agents to automatically accept calls, agents are presented with the same notification as in accepting calls but without an option to Decline. Also, shortly after receiving the notification the call is automatically accepted.

Declining calls

Calls are automatically routed to the first available agent. If that agent doesn't answer after a configurable amount of time, the call routes to the next available agent.

Don't wait for the allocated time to run out. Decline the call to send it straight to the next available agent so customers wait less time.

To decline a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details to view any associated contact attributes.
  2. Click Decline to reject the call and put it back in the queue.

articles.name: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.title: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.body:
When working with messaging conversations, there are two different ways to use tags:

  1. Client-side messaging metadata, set by the Web Widget or SDKs
  2. Tags added in an AI agent flow, using Sunshine Conversations CRM actions such as Add conversation tags

These two methods can look similar because both can affect the context of a conversation and the ticket created from it. However, they are designed for different use cases and behave differently over time.

This article explains how each option works, when to use it, and what to consider when deciding which one is best for your workflow.

This workflow includes the following sections:

  • Understanding the difference
  • Tagging options for messaging conversations
  • Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations
  • When to use each tagging method
  • Additional resources

Understanding the difference

Client-side messaging metadata

Client-side messaging metadata is set from the frontend, such as:

  • the Web Widget loaded on a website
  • an SDK implementation

With this method, your developer sends metadata from the client into the messaging conversation. This can include conversation fields and conversation tags.

For tags, the Web Widget or SDK sends values using the messaging metadata APIs. In the conversation metadata, this appears as zen:ticket:tags.

This method is useful when the information is already available in the client, such as:

  • the product page the customer is viewing
  • an order number already displayed in the app
  • the customer’s current cart state
  • a known context that should be passed into the ticket

Adding tags to the conversation via a CRM action

This represents the CRM action in AI Advanced Agents that can be applied at different levels (see Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents).

This method is useful when the tag should be determined by the logic of the AI agent conversation itself, for example:

  • the user went through a specific path in a Dialogue or Procedure
  • the bot identified a use case during the conversation
  • the bot collected values that should be reflected in the conversation or downstream ticket

Although both methods use tags, they are not the same mechanism.

Tagging options for messaging conversations

Option 1: Using client-side messaging metadata

If you want to pass tags from the Web Widget or from a mobile SDK, use the client-side messaging metadata APIs.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag value is known when the conversation begins
  • the tag comes from the page or app context
  • you want to pass context into the conversation before a ticket is created
  • you want to manage tag values from the frontend

Examples include:

  • tagging a conversation based on the page category
  • tagging a conversation with a product family
  • tagging a conversation based on the customer’s logged-in experience

Benefits of client-side metadata tags

Client-side metadata tags are a good fit when the tag is tied to the customer’s current session context in the widget or app.

Benefits include:

  • The data can be set automatically without asking the customer to provide it
  • The tags can be set as soon as the page or app state is known
  • The data can be cleared using the messaging metadata APIs when the frontend context changes
  • This approach is well suited to information that is only relevant to the current client-side experience

Option 2: Using tags in an AI agent flow

If you want the bot to decide when tags should be added during a conversation, use the Add conversation tags CRM action in your AI agent flow.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag depends on what happens during the bot flow
  • the tag should be added after the user answers a question
  • the tag reflects the conversation path or intent
  • the tag is part of your AI agent logic rather than your frontend implementation

Examples include:

  • adding a tag after the customer selects a support topic
  • adding a tag when the AI agent identifies a billing or return scenario
  • adding a tag to influence downstream routing or reporting

Benefits of AI agent flow tags

Using Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow is a good fit when the tag should be controlled by the bot itself.

Benefits include:

  • Tags can be added based on conversation logic
  • Tags can use values collected during the flow
  • Tags can be applied consistently across channels where the AI agent is used
  • Tags can help downstream routing, reporting, or field selection logic

Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations

When using AI agent flow tagging with Sunshine Conversations, it is important to understand that information stored on a Sunshine Conversations conversation persists on that conversation.

In Sunshine Conversations, a conversation is a persistent object. Metadata and tags added to that conversation remain associated with it until they are changed or overwritten.

Because of this, Add conversation tags should be used with a clear understanding of how long the tag should remain attached to the conversation.

This leads to two valid implementation choices.

Choice 1: The tag should persist on the conversation

In some workflows, persistent tagging is the correct behavior.

Use this option when the tag is meant to remain associated with the conversation as part of the intended workflow, such as:

  • identifying the overall type of conversation
  • preserving a long-lived classification for future use
  • supporting downstream handling across the same Sunshine Conversations conversation

Choice 2: The tag is only valid for the current session

In other workflows, the tag is only relevant to the current session or a specific moment in the flow.

In this case, you should take steps to prevent the tag from carrying over unintentionally in the same Sunshine Conversations conversation.

A practical way to do this is to use the CRM action Overwrite conversation tags to clear the existing tags where needed.

For example, you can:

  • clear the conversation tags at the beginning of the flow
  • clear them at a specific point before applying new session-specific tags
  • clear them whenever you want to avoid carrying over metadata from an earlier interaction

If needed, an empty value can be submitted using a space character (" ") to effectively clear the existing conversation tags in the flow.

This approach is useful when tags are intended to be session-scoped rather than conversation-scoped.

When to use each tagging method

Client-side messaging metadata

Choose client-side messaging metadata when:

  • the tag value is known in the Web Widget or SDK
  • the information comes from the page or app context
  • the tag should be managed from the frontend
  • the context is tied to the current widget or app experience

Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow

Choose Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow when:

  • the bot should decide when to add the tag
  • the tag depends on user responses or flow logic
  • the tag is part of the AI agent workflow
  • persistent conversation-level tagging is acceptable or intended

If you use Add conversation tags for information that is only valid for the current session, plan how to clear or overwrite those tags so they do not carry over unexpectedly.

The most important distinction is behavior over time:

  • Client-side metadata is designed around frontend-managed context
  • Sunshine Conversations conversation tags persist on the conversation until they are changed or overwritten

Choosing the right method depends on whether the tag should reflect client context or bot flow logic, and whether the tag should be temporary or persistent.

Additional resources

If you would like to learn more, see:

  • Using messaging metadata with the Zendesk Web Widget and SDKs
  • About configured actions for AI agents
  • Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents
  • Available CRM actions for AI agents and Sunshine Conversations
  • Sunshine Conversations key concepts

articles.name: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.title: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.body:
Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?

Explore reports auto assist activity based on agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, not every time auto assist triggers or generates content.

A ticket is included in the current auto assist reporting only when an agent interacts with a suggestion, for example, when an agent performs one of these tasks:

  • Accepts it
  • Edits it
  • Dismisses it

If auto assist generates a suggestion but the agent does not interact with it, that activity is not included in the available Copilot auto assist dataset.

This also means some tickets do not appear in reporting even though auto assist was active on the ticket, for example:

  • A suggestion was still being generated and auto assist was turned off before the suggestion was available
  • A suggestion was generated, but it did not contain any actionable content for the agent to interact with
  • A suggestion was generated, but the agent did not use or respond to it

Because of this, auto assist metrics in Explore can be lower than counts from ticket searches or other reports with the agent_copilot_enabled ticket tag. Those sources can show where auto assist is turned on, triggered, or generated a suggestion, while Explore reflects only suggestions that result in recorded agent interaction.

The current auto assist reporting is a measure of agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, rather than a complete count of every auto assist trigger, generated suggestion, or ticket where a procedure applies.

articles.name: Managing external knowledge connections
articles.title: Managing external knowledge connections
articles.body:
Once you'vecreated a knowledge connection, you can view information about the connection, set viewer permissions for content, and manually resync or remove the connection.

What's my plan?
All Suites Team, Growth, Professional, Enterprise, or Enterprise Plus
Support with Knowledge Professional or Enterprise

Summary: ◀▼

You can manage external knowledge connections by viewing connection details, setting viewer permissions, resyncing content, or removing a connection. Check sync status, last synced time, and item count, then choose who can view connected content with user segments or make it visible to everyone. You can also trigger a manual sync when content changes.

Once you've created a knowledge connection, you can view information about the connection, set viewer permissions for content, and manually resync or remove the connection.

This article contains the following topics:
  • Viewing connection details
  • Managing viewer permissions for content
  • Manually resyncing content
  • Removing a connection

Viewing connection details

You can use the information panel to view information about the connection, when it was created and by whom, and when it was last synced.

To view connection details
  1. In Knowledge admin, click Manage articles () in the sidebar.
  2. Click External content > Connections.
  3. Click the options menu () on the row for the connection you want to manage, then select Manage.

  4. View details about the connection:
    • Created: When the connection was created
    • Connected by: Name of the Knowledge admin who created the connection
    • Items: Number of synced pages in the connection
    • Status: Sync status
    • Last synced: When the last sync occurred. Syncs are automatically performed every 24 hours, though they can also be triggered manually

Managing viewer permissions for content

You can set the viewer permissions for who can view connected content in your Zendesk workflows.

To manage viewer permissions for content
  1. In Knowledge admin, click Manage articles () in the sidebar.
  2. Click External content > Connections.
  3. Click the options menu () on the row for the connection you want to manage, then select Manage.
  4. Under Viewing permissions, select one of the following options to determine which user segments can view content from this space:
    • Only visible to selected user segments: Select up to 10 user segments from any of the following (an Enterprise plan is required to select multiple user segments):
      • Signed-in users: Includes internal and external users who create an account and sign in to your help center.
      • Agents and admins: Includes team members only, so that you can create content that is internal-only.
        Note: Light agents are included in this segment. For a list of light agent permissions, see Understanding and setting light agent permissions.
      • Custom user segment: Allows you to restrict viewing access to specific users based on tags, organizations, or groups by applying user segments. See Creating user segments to restrict access.
    • Visible to everyone: Includes anyone who visits your help center and does not require sign in.
  5. Click Save.

Manually resyncing content

When you connect an external content source, a sync process runs every 24 hours to update new or changed content. However, you can also manually resync content to trigger a sync process at any time.

To manually resync content
  1. On the Connections page, click the options menu () for the connection you want to sync.
  2. Select Sync again.

    The connection syncs immediately, and the Last synced column is updated with the most recent sync time.

Removing a connection

You can remove a connection if you no longer want to sync its content. If you remove a connection, all content within that connection will be removed.

When you remove a connection, the related content is no longer available wherever external content is used.

To remove a connection
  1. On the Connections page, click the options menu () for the connection you want to remove.
  2. Select Remove connection.

  3. Review the message, then click Remove.

    The connection is removed from the Connection list, and is no longer available as an external content source.

articles.name: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.title: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When you try adding an email address to your Support addresses, or bulk update users with a comma-separated values (CSV) file, you receive an error or an email with a link to download an Excel file: Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email.

Resolving the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error

This error means you are trying to use an email address already used by another user on your account. That account may have been created by an email forwarded to your Support address.

To resolve the This email is already taken error:

  1. First, verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support with the email address from the error and confirm which user account already is associated with that email address
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change
  4. A final option if you have multiple users is to merge your users

For more information, see this article: Bulk importing users.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I receive the error "This email is already taken. Try another email"? What does the error "Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email" mean? Why can I not add an email address or update users?

This error means another user or customer account on your Zendesk Support help desk already uses that email address. An email forwarded to your Support address sometimes creates that duplicate account.

How do I resolve the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error? How do I fix the user couldn't be updated error when bulk updating users or adding a Support address? How can I repair duplicate email address conflicts?

  1. Verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it.
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support or your ticketing system with the email address from the error and confirm which user or customer account already is associated with that email address.
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change.
  4. Merge your users or team members if you have multiple users.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.title: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.body:
Summary

On June 19, 2026, between 14:19 UTC and 15:01 UTC, AI Agents customers across all pods experienced service delays and errors when using AI features such as composing and enhancing messages, due to an issue affecting the Open AI service in the GCP EU region.

Timeline

June 19, 2026 16: UTC | June 19, 2026 9:30 AM PT

We are aware of an issue that affected AI Agents and Zendesk Support AI features between 14:09 and 15:01 UTC on June 19. The incident was caused by a service degradation with our vendor, OpenAI, resulting in slow responses or intermittent errors for some customers. This issue has been fully resolved, and all services are operating normally. We apologize for the inconvenience caused.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an upstream service disruption in the EU region that impacted the flow of data through our system, resulting in cascading failures that affected AI agent performance.

Resolution

The issue was resolved by monitoring until the external service stabilized and recovered, after which related systems resumed normal operation without the need for manual intervention.

Remediation Items

  1. Explore options for automatic failover with the API provider to minimize service disruptions.
  2. Investigate why the on-call team was not contacted by phone during this incident and improve notification processes.
  3. Replace the global fallback setting with service-specific fallback configurations for better reliability.
  4. Confirm whether the fallback option for the Azure OpenAI service is properly configured.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - SunCo | All Pods - Message sending issues in WhatsApp, Facebook and Instagram Messenger
articles.title: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - SunCo | All Pods - Message sending issues in WhatsApp, Facebook and Instagram Messenger
articles.body:
Summary

On June 12, 2026, between 13:33 UTC and 16:30 UTC, AIR/Premier customers across multiple pods experienced an outage impacting messaging on Meta channels, including WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Instagram Messenger. The majority of the reported issues came from WhatsApp users in the LATAM region.

Timeline

June 12, 2026 14:13 UTC | June 12, 2026 7:13 AM PT

We are aware of an issue impacting social messaging channels including WhatsApp, Facebook messenger and Instagram messenger. We are investigating.

June 12, 2026 14:23 UTC | June 12, 2026 7:23 AM PT

Our engineers are investigating the issue impacting social messaging channels. At the moment this appears to be a widespread Meta outage, and we will provide further updates when we hear more information from Meta. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

June 12, 2026 14:55 UTC | June 12, 2026 7:55 AM PT

We are still working with Meta on resolving the issue impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram messaging. We will provide an update as soon as we have new information to share.

June 12, 2026 16:27 UTC | June 12, 2026 9:27 AM PT

We unfortunately do not have any substantive updates to share from Meta while we monitor the situation impacting Meta social messaging channels. We will be sure to share any news we can as soon as it is available.

June 12, 2026 17:37 UTC | June 12, 2026 10:37 AM PT

We are pleased to hear that Meta has resolved the issue that was impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram social messaging channels. Our own monitoring shows full recovery. Thank you.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a significant disruption on Meta’s platform, which prevented messages from being sent and received through WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Instagram Messenger.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, we escalated the outage to Meta support as soon as their portal became accessible. Throughout the incident, we kept customers informed via status updates and direct communications. We continuously monitored system performance until Meta restored service, and we confirmed full recovery before closing the incident.

Remediation Items

No remediations in place as the issue originated from our partner provider.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 22, 2026 - Support | Multiple Pods - Support dashboard blank page errors and login failures
articles.title: Service Incident - June 22, 2026 - Support | Multiple Pods - Support dashboard blank page errors and login failures
articles.body:
Summary

On June 22, 2026, between 13:33 UTC and 14:23 UTC, AIR/Premier Support customers across multiple pods, including Pods 19 and 23, experienced difficulties accessing the Support dashboard, encountering blank or error pages and intermittent login failures.

Timeline

June 22, 2026 14:15 UTC | June 22, 2026 7:15 AM PT

We are receiving reports of issues affecting agents' ability to log into the Support dashboard on pods 19 and 23, and our team is investigating. We will provide additional updates shortly.

June 22, 2026 14:29 UTC | June 22, 2026 7:29 AM PT

We have confirmed an issue with our CDN provider preventing Support agents from accessing the Support dashboard across multiple pods, and our team is investigating. We will continue to provide updates as we learn more.

June 22, 2026 14:42 UTC | June 22, 2026 7:42 AM PT

We have initiated failover from the regions affected by the ongoing CDN provider issue, and are seeing improvement in agents' ability to access Support. If you are experiencing issues, please refresh your browser and let us know if the issue persists. We will continue to post substantive updates as the investigation progresses.

June 22, 2026 17:24 UTC | June 22, 2026 10:24 AM PT

We are happy to report that we have resolved the issue affecting access to the Support product across multiple pods, and Support functionality is restored at this time. Thank you for your patience during our investigation.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an outage with our CDN provider that disrupted connectivity between their network and our Support dashboard servers, resulting in errors and blank pages or login failures for affected agents.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, we coordinated with our CDN provider and initiated a temporary traffic reroute to reduce errors while the provider addressed the incident. Once stability was confirmed, traffic was switched back to the normal route, and the alternative path was secured.

Remediation Items

  1. Investigate why the system did not trigger a failover alert during the Chicago Cloudflare outage to improve future detection and response.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.
